codeReview.summary: The commit correctly adds the Settings-only third relay, mono header typography, sidebar/scrim z-index layering, and peer trailing icons gated by sidebar state. However, it still renders a Rotate Keyset row inside the Settings sidebar, which conflicts with the contract for this feature and with the assigned requirement to keep Rotate Keyset/Recover out of the sidebar.

issues.description: Settings sidebar still includes a "Rotate Keyset" action row/button. For this feature, the sidebar should contain Device Profile, Group Profile, Rotate Share, Export & Backup, and Profile Security content only (no Rotate Keyset/Recover rows in the sidebar).
VAL-DSH-010 test asserts section labels/order but does not assert absence of non-contract rows (e.g., Rotate Keyset), allowing the parity violation to pass tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature metadata is internally inconsistent about sidebar row scope, which likely contributed to retaining Rotate Keyset in the sidebar.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json: description for dashboard-settings-sidebar-fidelity says NOT Rotate Keyset/Recover in sidebar, while expectedBehavior still lists Rotate Keyset and Recover rows (lines ~1123 vs ~1130 in mission features.json).

summary: Review failed due to a blocking parity issue: Rotate Keyset is still rendered in the Settings sidebar. Other requested changes in this commit (three-relay sidebar list, mono header, peer trailing icon gating, scrim/sidebar z-index and click-outside close) are implemented.
